name: Chilled Pea Pod Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Snap ends and remove strings from pea pods.
2. Melt butter in a heavy large saucepan over medium heat.
3. Saute pods and onions until onions are tender, about 5 minutes.
4. Add broth and minced tarragon and bring to boil.
5. Reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
6. Mix in romaine and simmer for 5 minutes.
7. Puree the soup as finely as possible in batches using a processor on/off turns, about 5 minutes.
8. Strain through a fine sieve into a bowl.
9. Stir in half cup creme fraiche.
10. Season with salt, pepper, sugar or lemon juice if desired.
11. Cool to room temperature.
12. Cover and refrigerate until well chilled, at least 4 hours.
13. (Can be prepared up to 1 day ahead.)
14. Just before serving, ladle soup into individual bowls.
15. Top with a dollop of creme fraiche and fresh tarragon sprig.
16. Garnish with fresh peas if desired.
